Home / Compare / vs Power BI
Compare

DataVision Pro vs Power BI

Updated 2026-04-15

DataVision Pro is a browser-first analytics tool focused on uploading a file and getting cleaned data, statistics, ML, and AI insights immediately. Power BI is Microsoft's full BI platform, deeply integrated with Excel, Microsoft 365, and Azure. Pick DataVision Pro for speed and AI; pick Power BI for ecosystem fit inside a Microsoft shop.

Best for DataVision Pro

Teams that live in spreadsheets and want AI-explained analysis without modelling a dataset first.

Best for Power BI

Organisations standardised on Microsoft 365 / Azure that need DAX-powered, governed BI across the company.

Side-by-side comparison

FeatureDataVision ProPower BI
File upload analysis Drag CSV/XLSX → instant analysis Power Query → load → model → visualise
Cleaning pipeline Toggleable, reorderable substeps Power Query (M language)
AI insights GPT-powered chat + auto report Copilot for Power BI (separate licence) [verify]
Predictive models Built-in linear / RandomForest / K-Means Requires Azure ML or R/Python visuals
Microsoft 365 integration Email exports + CSV downloads Native — Teams, SharePoint, Excel
Mobile editing Browser-based, responsive View on mobile; authoring is desktop-only (Windows)
Pricing entry point 60-day free Tier 3 trial Power BI Pro per-user / Premium per-capacity
Learning curve No DAX, no formula language DAX + M required for non-trivial work

When to choose DataVision Pro

  • Your data lives in spreadsheets and you want results without a modelling step.
  • You want AI summaries and recommendations baked into every chart.
  • You're not on a Windows desktop, or you work cross-platform.
  • You don't have time to learn DAX.

When to choose Power BI

  • You're a Microsoft 365 / Azure shop with existing Power BI investment.
  • You need DAX-grade modelling and row-level security at enterprise scale.
  • Your reports must live next to Teams, SharePoint, and Excel.
  • You have a BI team that already speaks DAX and M.

Frequently asked questions

Can I open a Power BI .pbix in DataVision Pro?

No. DataVision Pro consumes CSV and Excel files directly. Export your Power BI dataset to CSV/XLSX to bring it in.

Does DataVision Pro need Windows?

No. It runs in any modern browser on Mac, Linux, ChromeOS, Windows, and tablets.

Is Power BI free?

Power BI Desktop is free; sharing and collaboration require Power BI Pro or Premium licences. [verify against current Microsoft pricing]

Which has better AI?

Both ship AI features. DataVision Pro builds AI into every analysis page out of the box; Power BI Copilot is powerful but typically requires additional licences and a Premium capacity.

Notes marked [verify] are pending confirmation against the latest official pricing or feature pages from Power BI. We never publish a number we can't source.

Try DataVision Pro on your own data

60-day Tier 3 trial. No credit card.

An intelligent data analytics platform that turns raw datasets into clear, actionable insights — in seconds, no code required.
